ksas.statement: Knowledge of clinical data management principles, including Good Clinical Practice requirements, ICH E6(R2) guidelines, and the regulatory expectations for data integrity, audit trails, and traceability in clinical trial submissions.

Knowledge of electronic data capture systems and clinical data management platforms, including database design, edit check programming, query management workflows, and the validation requirements for 21 CFR Part 11 and Annex 11 compliance.

Knowledge of clinical data standards, including CDISC standards (CDASH, SDTM, ADaM), MedDRA coding conventions, WHO Drug Dictionary, and the submission dataset requirements for FDA and EMA regulatory filings.
Knowledge of data quality dimensions (accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness, uniqueness, validity, integrity) and the measurement approaches applicable to each dimension.
Knowledge of data quality management program design, including profiling methodology, rule authoring standards, threshold setting, issue classification, and escalation path design.
Knowledge of root cause analysis methods for data quality failures, including source system analysis, transformation logic review, and process failure identification.
Knowledge of MDM architectural patterns, including registry, consolidation, coexistence, and centralized hub models, and the organizational and technical conditions that favor each.
Knowledge of entity resolution methods, including deterministic matching rules, probabilistic matching, and ML-based matching, and the precision-recall trade-offs involved.
Knowledge of reference data management principles, including reference data taxonomy, governance of controlled vocabularies, and change management for reference data updates.
Knowledge of metadata types (business, technical, operational, process) and the systems and methods used to collect, store, and maintain each type.
Knowledge of data catalog platform capabilities, including automated metadata harvesting, lineage visualization, search and discovery, classification, and glossary linkage.
Knowledge of data lineage concepts and documentation methods, including end-to-end lineage from source to consumption, transformation-level lineage, and impact analysis use cases.
Knowledge of data minimization, purpose limitation, consent management, and legitimate interest assessment as operational data management practices.
Knowledge of data product design principles, including data product definition, consumer segmentation, interface design (APIs, subscriptions, files), SLA definition, and data product lifecycle.
Knowledge of data mesh principles and the role of data product thinking within a federated data ownership model.
Knowledge of product management practices as applied to data products, including roadmap prioritization, consumer discovery, feedback loops, and value measurement.
Knowledge of data quality validation techniques and how to embed quality checks into pipeline execution with appropriate failure handling and alerting.

Knowledge of data quality testing frameworks, including schema validation, data contract enforcement, freshness checks, volume anomaly detection, and referential integrity verification within automated pipelines.
Knowledge of master data management (MDM) principles, including golden record construction, data domain definition, MDM hub architectures, and stewardship workflows.
Knowledge of data quality management frameworks, including DQ dimensions (accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness, uniqueness, validity), measurement approaches, and remediation workflows.
Knowledge of data quality dimensions and how to assess, measure, and document quality issues within a specific data domain.
Skill in identifying and communicating data quality issues that affect analytical conclusions, including null handling, outlier treatment, and source system reliability caveats.

Skill in managing clinical data quality processes, including medical coding review, query resolution, discrepancy management, and the reconciliation of clinical data across electronic data capture, safety databases, and central laboratory systems.
Skill in designing and implementing data quality measurement programs, including profiling, rule definition, scoring, and business impact quantification.
Skill in producing data quality reporting for business and governance audiences, translating technical quality scores into business risk and operational impact language.
Skill in designing matching and merging rules for entity resolution, including rule documentation, threshold calibration, and exception handling workflows.
Skill in operating a data catalog platform, including configuring connectors, reviewing harvested metadata, curating business context, and managing access.
Skill in conducting data consumer discovery, translating consumer data needs into data product requirements with defined quality, freshness, and access standards.
Skill in defining and managing data product SLAs, including freshness, quality, availability, and access response time commitments.
Skill in communicating data quality and data trust issues to non-technical executive stakeholders in terms of business risk and operational impact.
Skill in identifying, documenting, and escalating data quality issues through established governance processes with appropriate business impact context.

Manage ongoing data quality during clinical trials, including query generation and resolution, serious adverse event reconciliation, medical coding review, and database lock preparation with documented clean-point verification.
Produce clinical data management documentation, including data management plans, data review plans, CRF completion guidelines, and database lock reports required for regulatory submission packages.
Conduct exploratory data analysis on new datasets, documenting distributional characteristics, data quality findings, and implications for modeling approach.
Define and maintain enterprise data quality standards, including dimensional targets, measurement methodology, scoring approach, and escalation thresholds by data domain.
Operate the data quality issue management process, including intake, prioritization, root cause assignment, remediation tracking, and closure verification.
Produce regular data quality reports for governance and business stakeholders, including trend analysis, high-impact issue summaries, and remediation progress.
Manage the MDM hub operations, including golden record maintenance, match review queues, merge/unmerge workflows, and survivorship rule updates.
Maintain the enterprise data catalog, including asset registration, metadata quality review, business context enrichment, and freshness monitoring.
Document and maintain data lineage for critical data elements and high-risk data flows, ensuring lineage is current and accessible to authorized consumers.
Define and maintain the data product roadmap, including consumer prioritization, capability dependencies, delivery milestones, and deprecation plans.
Manage the data product lifecycle, including launch, active management, deprecation, and consumer migration planning.
Monitor data product health against defined SLAs, managing consumer communications for SLA breaches and planned changes.
Implement and maintain data quality validation checks within pipelines, documenting quality rules, threshold definitions, and remediation procedures.
Implement and operate data observability infrastructure that provides pipeline lineage, execution history, data quality metrics, and proactive alerting for anomalies and SLA violations.
Define and enforce enterprise data quality standards, including dimensional targets, measurement processes, issue triage procedures, and escalation thresholds.
Monitor data quality within the assigned domain, document issues, initiate remediation workflows, and track resolution to closure.

Ability to prioritize data quality investments based on business impact, focusing remediation efforts on data quality issues that most directly affect organizational decision-making and operational outcomes.

Ability to distinguish between data quality issues that require systemic remediation at the source system and those that can be appropriately addressed through transformation logic, documentation, or consumer guidance.
Ability to build organizational commitment to data quality by connecting quality metrics to tangible business outcomes rather than relying on abstract data management principles.
Ability to design data product interfaces that serve diverse consumer needs across different technical proficiency levels without creating unsustainable maintenance burden.
